title: Remove Notifications

posts.author: Molina (Irene)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have several items assigned to external guests. We interact with them through the dashboard. They must upload certain documentation.

On the other hand, these same items are also assigned to internal teams.

We want the internal team to receive an email when a status changes, but the external guest shouldn’t receive anything.

Is this possible? I’ve disabled notifications for the external guest’s profile, but they still receive notifications about status changes for the assigned item.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s definitely possible. It sounds like you might have automations turned on to notify the users, can you check that first and let me know what you see?

posts.author: jdandre-orangedot (Julian D'Andre)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Irene!

This is a great question and one that is relatively common. I have two suggestions for what you can do here:

  1. Use a status to email map to send out notifications. The automation might look something like this: "When status changes to X, send email to [Email Column]. Optionally, use another column, like User Type, to help filter who receives what. That way you can leave the email column blank for items involving external guests.

  2. Avoid using the people column for external subscribers. If someone is assigned to a task via the people column, they will always receive a notification. Instead, use a custom text or email column to store their names or contact info.

This stops guests from automatically receiving updates—critical if you’re sharing sensitive or staged info internally.

I hope this helps!

Julian D’Andre

orangedotdigital.com

OrangeDot - monday.com Platinum Partner

posts.author: Molina (Irene)
posts.cookedHtml:
I need to use the People column for external subscribers because they log into the dashboard to upload required documentation.

Indeed, I use the email column to store their contact information and create the automation with Gmail.

What I need is, even though they’re assigned to the People column, they don’t receive notifications about status changes in certain columns.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Click on ‘automate’ at the top of the board

image563×150 7.21 KB

Then click on ‘board automations’

image563×150 4.65 KB

I expect that you’ll have one like this

image603×306 12 KB

posts.author: Molina (Irene)
posts.cookedHtml:
The automations that external guests receive aren’t because of created automations. Is because they’re assigned to the person column. Therefore, the solution is on the other way.

Thanks anyway.

posts.author: Ozi (Oz Israel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Irene,

  1. On the board level, click on the three dots at the upper right corner > press permissions. Here, an external guest can choose which notification to receive from the board.
  2. Another option is for the guest to go to their profile settings (by pressing the avatar at the upper right corner) > My profile > notifications. Here, each guest can choose which notification to receive.
    The first option applies to a specific board level, and the second applies to the entire account.

Hope that helps,
Oz
